conbody.p: Overview: Encounter Monitoring report enables the CDI Manager to manage the daily workload of the CDS team by analyzing unassigned encounters, concurrent encounters, not-reconciled and all encounters.

The report provides answers to the following business questions.
What does encounter distribution by status, unit, or payer look like? Does any status look like it’s becoming a problem? Is the team falling behind with reconciliation?
How many cases are unassigned? If yes, do any of my CDSs have the capacity to pick these cases up?
If cases are unassigned, what was the payer breakdown? Were any Medicare cases left unassigned?
Is there an opportunity to level out case assignments, i.e., can work be distributed across team members to help cover caseload?
How many discharged cases are unassigned?
Navigation: On the Analytics home page left side panel, navigate to CDE One CDI Operations Encounter Monitoring .

Encounter Monitoring displays the following:
KPI Card: Encounters
Encounter Count by Payer Chart
Encounter Count by Unit Chart
Encounter Count by Status Chart
Encounter Count by CDS Chart
On the top panel, you can select desired values for the slicers (refer ) and the corresponding data gets filtered in the charts.
For filters all pages, refer .
For View, refer .
For Info icon, refer
For Footnotes, refer .
This KPI Card displays the total count of encounters based on the 'View' selected.
Example: If you select 'Concurrent Encounters' from the 'View' dropdown list, this card displays the total count of concurrent encounters.

p.b: Encounter Monitoring - Encounter Count by Payer Chart

li.p: This chart displays the count of encounters for each payer, depending upon the View selected.
Example: When you select ‘Unassigned Encounters’ from the 'View' drop-down list, this chart displays the count of Unassigned encounters for each payer.
Encounters that do not have a payer assigned are displayed under the 'Unassigned Payer' category.

ul.li: X-axis: Displays the Payer in the format Payer Description
Y-axis: Displays the count of encounters.
Sort: Sort by Payer with the highest count of encounters to the payer with the lowest count of encounters (descending order).

p.b: Encounter Monitoring - Encounter Count by Unit Chart

li.p: This chart displays the count of encounters for each Unit, depending upon the measure selected.
Example: When you select ‘Unassigned Encounters’ from the 'View' drop-down list, this chart displays the count of Unassigned encounters for each Unit.
Encounters that do not have a Unit assigned are displayed under the 'Unassigned Unit' category.

ul.li: X-axis: Displays the Unit.
Y-axis: Displays the count of encounters.
Sort: Sort by Unit with the highest count of encounters to the Unit with the lowest count of encounters (descending order).

p.b: Encounter Monitoring - Encounter Count by Status Chart

li.p: This chart displays the count of encounters for each Status, depending upon the View selected.
Example: When you select the Unassigned from the 'View' drop-down list, this chart displays the count of Unassigned encounters for each Status.

Applicable statuses are displayed based on the selected measure. The following encounter statuses are applicable for each measure.
View Encounter statuses
Unassigned Encounters Discharged, Untouched
Assigned Encounters In Progress, Reconciled - Impact, Discharged, Auto Reconciled, Reconciled - No Impact, Review Complete, Untouched, Ready to Reconcile, Reconciliation Hold
Assigned and Not Reviewed Discharged, In Progress, Untouched, Renconciled - No Impact, Review Complete.
Assigned and Reviewed In Progress, Reconciled - Impact, Discharged, Auto Reconciled, Reconciled - No Impact, Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, Reconciliation Hold
Concurrent Encounters In Progress, Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, Reconciliation Hold
Non-Reconciled Encounters Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, Reconciliation Hold
Reconciled Encounters Reconciled-Impact, Reconciled No Impact, Auto-Reconciled

ul.li: X-axis: Displays the Encounter Status.
Y-axis: Displays the count of encounters.
Sort: Sort by Status with the highest count of encounters to the Status with the lowest count of encounters (descending order).

p.b: Encounter Monitoring - Encounter Count by CDS Chart

li.p: This chart displays the count of encounters for each CDS, depending upon the View selected.
Example: When you select ‘Unassigned Encounters’ from the 'View' drop-down list, this chart displays the count of Unassigned encounters for each CDS.
Encounters that do not have a CDS assigned are displayed under the 'Unassigned CDS' category.

ul.li: X-axis: Displays the CDS in the format CDS Name (CDS ID)
Y-axis: Displays the count of encounters.
Sort: Sort by CDS with the highest count of encounters to the CDS with the lowest count of encounters (descending order).
Displays the admit date range here. You can select required dates from the date lookup.
Default: Last 1 calendar months date range selected by default.
Displays the discharge date range here. You can select required dates from the date lookup.
With the Reset button, you can reset the admit date range.
Tooltip: Use "Ctrl + Click" to reset the Admit Date and Discharge Date.

Displays the list of View - Unassigned Encounters, Assigned Encounters, Assigned and Not Reviewed, Assigned and Reviewed, Non-Reconciled Encounters, Concurrent Encounters, Reconciled Encounters, All Encounters.
You can select only one View.
Default: Unassigned Encounters.
Displays a list of active and inactive facilities that you have access to. The list is sorted alphabetically.
You can select multiple facilities
You can search the facility by Facility ID or Description.
Format: Facility Description (Facility ID)
Default: ALL
Displays a list of active and inactive payers associated with the facilities that you have access to. The list is sorted alphabetically.
You can select multiple payers
"Unassigned Payer" displays data for the encounters that do not have a payer assigned.
You can search the Payer by Payer ID or Description.
Format: Payer Description (Payer ID)
Default: ALL

Displays the available patient types.
Example: Inpatient, Outpatient.
Default: Inpatient
Displays the list of available Visit Types.
You can select multiple Visit Types.
You can search the Visit Type.
Default: ALL
Displays the list of available units.
You can select multiple Units.
You can search the Unit.
Default: ALL
Displays Weekend Only Stay values (Y/N).
You can search the Weekend Only Stay.
Default: ALL
Displays the list of available Encounter Statuses.
You can select multiple Encounter Statuses.
You can search the Encounter Status.
Default: Review Not Needed unchecked by default.
Displays the list of available Admit Services.
You can select multiple Admit Services.
You can search the Admit Service.
Default: ALL

conbody.p: Right click on a bar, select Drill through option, and then select Encounter Details. You get navigated to the ‘Encounter Details’ tab.
The ‘Encounter Details’ tab displays all the encounters for the selected Payer.
Right click on a bar, select Drill through option, and then select Encounter Details. You get navigated to the ‘Encounter Details’ tab.
The ‘Encounter Details’ tab displays all the encounters for the selected Unit.
Right click on a bar, select Drill through option, and then select Encounter Details. You get navigated to the ‘Encounter Details’ tab.
The ‘Encounter Details’ tab displays all the encounters for the selected Encounter Status.

p.note: The data displayed in the report is based on the "View" selected. For example, if you have selected the view as Unassigned encounters and encounter status filter as "In Progress", the system considers only Untouched and Discharged cases and there will not be any data for "In Progress" cases.

conbody.p: Right click on a bar, select Drill through option, and then select Encounter Details. You get navigated to the ‘Encounter Details’ tab.
The ‘Encounter Details’ tab displays all the encounters that have the selected CDS assigned.

row.entry: The data for the encounters that have the status of 'Discharged' or 'Untouched' and do not have a CDS assigned.
Displays the data for all the encounters that have a CDS assigned.

entry.p: Displays the data for the assigned encounters with no Initial Review Date/Time.

row.entry: Displays the data for the assigned encounters with an Initial Review Date/Time.
The data for the encounters that have the status of Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, or Reconciliation Hold.
The data for the encounters that have the status of In Progress, Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, Reconciliation Hold.
The data for the encounters that have the status of Reconciled-Impact, Reconciled No Impact, Auto-Reconciled.

entry.p: The data for all the encounter statuses.

p.note: All encounter statuses including Review Not Needed encounter status is also considered under this category.

conbody.p:
The Info icon displays the below information based on the 'View' selected: Info icon
View Data Displayed on the Report
Unassigned Encounters The data for the encounters that have the status of 'Discharged' or 'Untouched' and do not have a CDS assigned gets displayed.
Assigned Encounters Displays the data for all the encounters that have a CDS assigned.
Assigned and Not Reviewed
Displays the data for the assigned encounters with no Initial Review Date/Time.
Assigned and Reviewed Displays the data for the assigned encounters with an Initial Review Date/Time.
Non-Reconciled Encounters The data for the encounters that have the status of Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, or Reconciliation Hold gets displayed.
Concurrent Encounters The data for the encounters that have the status of Untouched, Discharged, In Progress, Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, Reconciliation Hold gets displayed..
Reconciled Encounters The data for the encounters that have the status of Reconciled-Impact, Reconciled No Impact, Auto-Reconciled gets displayed.
All Encounters
The data for all the encounter statuses gets displayed.
Select ‘Review Not Needed’ status from the ‘Filters on all pages’ section to include RNN encounters for ‘All Encounters’ View.
The formula bar in the footnote displays the following formulas.

row.entry: Count of inpatient admissions/discharges including Review Not Needed as per the 'View' selected
Inpatient encounters with 'Discharged' or 'Untouched' status and do not have a CDS assigned.
Inpatient encounters that have a CDS assigned.

entry.p: Displays the data for the assigned encounters with no Initial Review Date/Time.

row.entry: Assigned inpatient encounters with an Initial Review Date/time.
Inpatient encounters with Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, or Reconciliation Hold encounter status.
Inpatient encounters with Untouched, Discharged, In Progress, Review Complete, Ready to Reconcile, Reconciliation Hold encounter status.
Inpatient encounters with Reconciled-Impact, Reconciled No Impact, Auto-Reconciled encounter status.
Inpatient encounters with all encounter statuses. Select ‘Review Not Needed’ status from the ‘Filters on all pages’ section to include RNN encounters for ‘All Encounters’ View.
